title: 10 hidden spots in Spain to see the rare sunset total solar eclipse on Aug. 12
summary: Spain will experience a rare sunset total solar eclipse on August 12, with hidden locations like desert badlands and hilltop villages offering optimal viewing. These off-the-beaten-track spots provide clear views of the celestial event.

title: Scientists created one of the largest simulations of our universe ever — about the size of 500,000 HD movies
summary: Scientists developed one of the largest universe simulations, equivalent to 500,000 HD movies, to study galaxy and cosmic structure evolution. The FLAMINGO project aims to explore dark matter's role over billions of years.

title: Get more for your credits with the best leftover Lego Star Wars Day deals
summary: Lego is offering discounts on Star Wars-themed sets for Star Wars Day. These deals include various sets like AT-TEs and Baby Yoda.

title: Solar activity makes space junk crash to Earth faster
summary: Scientists found that increased solar activity accelerates the rate at which space junk falls to Earth after analyzing orbital data over 36 years.

title: Mexico City is sinking up to 14 inches per year, satellite images show
summary: Mexico City is experiencing severe subsidence, sinking up to 14 inches per year according to satellite imagery. This makes it one of the fastest-sinking cities globally.

title: NASA just released 12,000 photos from Artemis 2. Here are our top picks
summary: NASA released 12,000 photos from the Artemis 2 moon mission, offering a detailed visual record of the historic flight. The images provide unprecedented insights into the mission's progress and preparations.

title: Watch SpaceX Starlink train circle Earth in amazing satellite video
summary: A newly launched SpaceX Starlink satellite captured a video showing its view as it orbits Earth. The footage provides a unique perspective of the planet from space.

title: A tiny 'plutino' world beyond Neptune has grown a mysterious atmosphere, and we don't know how
summary: A trans-Neptunian object, a tiny 'plutino' beyond Neptune, has developed an unexpected thin atmosphere after occulting a distant star. Astronomers are puzzled by the discovery and its unexplained origin.

title: Moon lander preps for the lunar surface | Space photo of the day for May 6, 2026
summary: Blue Origin's MK1 moon lander has successfully completed vacuum chamber tests. The development is part of NASA's efforts to return astronauts to the lunar surface.

title: 'SOL Shogunate' is a samurai space opera set on the moon, but with an aerospace engineer making sure it's 'getting the science right'
summary: The space opera 'SOL Shogunate' is set on the moon and features an aerospace engineer ensuring scientific accuracy. Chaos Manufacturing CEO Guy Costantini and aerospace engineer Taylore McClurg discuss the process of creating a believable yet visually striking lunar adventure.

title: Melting Snow Off Shivelyuch
summary: Near-constant volcanic activity is observed at Shiveluch in Russia, causing melting snow. This ongoing event poses potential risks to nearby areas.

title: NASA eClips and GLOBE Educators Strengthen a Regional STEM Ecosystem in Coastal Virginia
summary: NASA eClips and GLOBE Educators collaborated with educators and community organizations in Virginia’s Tidewater region to enhance STEM education. The partnership aims to strengthen regional STEM ecosystems through dynamic instructional practices.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Mars Rover Surveys ‘Crocodile Bridge’
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover captured a 360-degree panorama of a region called 'Crocodile Bridge' on Jezero Crater's rim. The image is composed of 980 images taken on December 18, 2025.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4879-4885: Struggle at Atacama
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover faces challenges while exploring Chile's Atacama Desert, one of the driest places on Earth. The mission team plans activities for sols 4879-4885 despite the harsh conditions.

title: 650 NASA Volunteers Have Co-Authored Scientific Papers
summary: Over 650 NASA volunteers have co-authored peer-reviewed scientific papers through NASA's citizen science projects, showcasing their significant contributions to research.

title: NASA’s Curiosity Rover Frees Its Drill From a Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover successfully freed its drill from a rock on Mars by waving its robotic arm and running the drill multiple times. The process was captured by the rover's hazard cameras.

title: NASA Astronaut to Answer Questions from Students in Florida
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams will answer STEM questions from Florida students during a live Earth-to-space call aboard the International Space Station on May 8. The event will be streamed on NASA's Learn With NASA YouTube channel.

title: NASA Research Shows Early Life Relied on Rare Metal
summary: NASA-funded research reveals that early life on Earth over 3 billion years ago depended on the rare metal molybdenum. The study, published in Nature Communications, is the first to demonstrate molybdenum's role in ancient biological processes.

title: Building on America’s 65-Year Legacy of Human Spaceflight
summary: On May 5, 1961, the Mercury-Redstone 3 mission launched Alan B. Shepard Jr. into space, making him the first American in space. This event marked a key milestone in America's 65-year legacy of human spaceflight.

title: NASA’S Juno Misson Captures Jupiter Moon Thebe
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured an image of Jupiter's moon Thebe during a close flyby on May 1, 2026. The Stellar Reference Unit (SRU) took the photo from 3,100 miles away with a resolution of 1.9 miles per pixel.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The process follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and a Chang Zheng 7 rocket launch. This may precede a busier period in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit aboard a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight with a revised trajectory. The company has issued notices for the upcoming test flight.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket for the first time on Saturday, marking a significant milestone for the country's space program. The maiden flight demonstrates progress in Russia's efforts to modernize its launch capabilities.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor selected as the next NASA astronaut for the ISS mission discusses their upcoming journey. The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and military service overseas.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's LEO satellites this week from multiple locations. Six launches are planned across Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test is essential for ensuring the parachute's reliability during a future mission.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an in-person event in Paris and an online preparatory session.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste has successfully transmitted its first navigation signal from an optimal location. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of its navigation system.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Astranis Raises $300M Series E
summary: Astranis has raised $300 million in a Series E funding round, valuing the company at $2.8 billion. The round was co-led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding to expand its operations. Plans include building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling the company's workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation. The funds will be used to scale operations, launch the constellation, and develop technology to meet increasing demand for Earth observation data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer, positioning itself as a central hub for such applications. This initiative aims to simplify the regulatory process for emerging space ventures.

title: Bridenstine Takes Over as Quantum Space CEO
summary: Jim Bridenstine, former NASA administrator, has been appointed CEO of Quantum Space, returning to his background in national security and defense. The move marks a shift from his previous role in space exploration.

title: Anduril Names Its SBI Team For Golden Dome
summary: Anduril has named its team for the Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) component of the Golden Dome missile defense architecture. The company is one of 12 prime contractors involved in this project.

title: ESA Taps Edge Aerospace for Space Cloud Contract
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has selected Edge Aerospace, a Luxembourg-based company, to develop an architecture and roadmap for orbital data centers under a new space cloud contract. The agreement aims to advance in-space computing and data processing capabilities.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force, potentially becoming its third chief of space operations since the branch's establishment in 2019.

title: Payload Field Guide: Lunar Rovers
summary: NASA and its partners are advancing lunar rover technology to support sustained human presence on the Moon, moving beyond Apollo-era designs to more capable vehicles. The focus is on developing rovers that can handle long-term surface operations and heavy payloads.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear executives are optimistic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which Administrator Isaacman views as a logical evolution for the agency. NASA is significantly supporting this initiative beyond just funding.

title: SWISSto12 Strikes German Partnership Deal for D2D GEO Satellite
summary: SWISSto12 has formed a new partnership in Germany to enhance media broadcast capabilities for Geostationary (GEO) satellites. The collaboration focuses on developing Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite technology.

title: Anduril to Work With Impulse Space, K2 Space, Voyager, and Others on Space-Based Interceptor Award
summary: Anduril Industries has partnered with Impulse Space, Inversion Space, K2 Space, and others to develop space-based interceptor technologies. The collaboration follows Anduril's recent award to prototype these systems.

title: Telesat’s GEO Business Declines 25% in Q1
summary: Telesat reported a 25% year-over-year decline in its GEO business for Q1 2026, as the company focuses on launching its Lightspeed constellation. CEO Dan Goldberg addressed the decline in the earnings report.

title: What Amazon’s Globalstar Acquisition Means for MSS Spectrum and D2D Market: Analyst Roundtable
summary: Amazon acquired Globalstar in a $10.8 billion deal, signaling a major move into the Mobile Satellite Services (MSS) spectrum and Direct-to-Device (D2D) market.

title: NRO Makes New Commercial Awards to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded new contracts for Earth sensing data to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel under its Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) effort. This expands the NRO's commercial partnerships for satellite-based data collection.

title: STMicroelectronics Expects its Space Business to Reach More than $3B Over Three Years
summary: STMicroelectronics anticipates its space business revenue to exceed $3 billion over the next three years. The company supplies semiconductors to SpaceX and other space industry players.

title: Lockheed Martin Joins Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to Develop Sea-Based Launch
summary: Lockheed Martin has joined Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to develop a sea-based launch platform. The collaboration aims to create an offshore launch system.

title: Future-Proofing Satellite Networks With ST Engineering iDirect Sales SVP Brian Jakins
summary: ST Engineering iDirect's Sales SVP Brian Jakins discusses strategies to future-proof satellite networks amid advancements like 5G. The article highlights the importance of adapting to new technologies for network operators.

title: Vantor Nabs $70 Million NGA Award For GEOINT Portal Use
summary: Vantor secured a $70 million award from the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) for the first option year of a GEOINT portal program. The contract supports the agency and vetted partners in geospatial intelligence operations.

title: Lynk, EarthDaily, True Anomaly Launch Satellites on Weekend SpaceX Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX launched 45 payloads, including satellites from Lynk, EarthDaily, and True Anomaly, on a rideshare mission carrying a South Korean Earth Observation satellite. This mission highlights the growing commercial space sector and SpaceX's role in facilitating multiple payload deployments.
